title: ulmus montana f. exoniensis
intro: Ulmus montana f. exoniensis, also known as the Exeter Elm, is a variety of the Wych Elm (Ulmus glabra) native to the UK. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Ulmus montana f. exoniensis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential allergies and interactions with medications.
